/Users/jason/.local/bin/devdash
dd: aliased to devdash
# DevDash CLI Reference

## Commands
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  version                              Print version

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"

Here's the full command set:

### Query Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `ready [--since=X]` | Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority (excludes thoughts) |
| `list [--status=X] [--since=X]` | All issues, optionally filtered |
| `blocked` | Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies |
| `show <id>` | Full issue detail (description, deps, parent) |
| `find <uuid>` | Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required) |
| `stats` | Project health: open/closed/blocked counts |
| `stale [--since=X]` | In-progress issues with no recent activity |

### Mutation Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `create --title="..." [flags]` | Create a new issue |
| `update <id> [flags]` | Update an issue |
| `close <id> [<id>...] [flags]` | Close one or more issues |
| `delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]` | Delete issues |
| `report <id> --status=X [flags]` | Report agent progress |

### Relationship & Comment Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `dep add <issue> <depends-on>` | Add a dependency |
| `dep remove <issue> <depends-on>` | Remove a dependency |
| `comment <id> --body="..."` | Add a comment |
| `comments <id>` | List comments |
| `activity [<id>]` | View activity log |

### Setup & Utility Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `login [--no-browser]` | Authenticate |
| `init` | Initialize project in current repo |
| `doctor` | Check configuration |
| `team` | List team members |
| `prime` | Output agent workflow context |
| `version` | Print version |

### ID Formats

| Format | Example |
|--------|---------|
| Full UUID | `27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4` |
| Short prefix | `27bf` (any unique prefix) |
| Local ID | `dev-dash-42` (project-scoped) |

### Priority Levels

`0`=critical, `1`=high, `2`=medium (default), `3`=low, `4`=backlog

message.content: what's up with "report" vs "update"?

content.text:
Good question. They serve different purposes:

**`update`** — Changes issue metadata (status, title, priority, assignee, etc.). It's a direct mutation of the issue itself. You'd use this to mark something `in_progress`, change priority, reassign, etc.

**`report`** — Logs a progress checkpoint *against* an issue without necessarily changing it. It's fire-and-forget status signaling (e.g., "code complete", "committed", "pushed", "error") with an optional summary. Think of it as appending to an activity log rather than editing the issue.

Key differences:
- `update` changes the issue's state
- `report` records a point-in-time status update (like a breadcrumb trail of progress)
- `report` is described as "won't fail your workflow" — it's designed to be safe for agents to call without risking a failure that blocks the next step

In practice: `update --status=in_progress` when you start work, then `report --status=code_complete` when code is done, `report --status=pushed` after push, and finally `close` when everything lands.

message.content: hm, do we have any agent instructions around how to use "report"?

content.text:
Yes — there's a brief mention in `CLAUDE.md`:

```
Report at milestones: `devdash report <id> --status=code_complete|committed|pushed|error --summary="..."`
```

And the help topic reference:

```
- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values
```

Want me to pull the full `devdash help report` output to see what the detailed guidance says? That might reveal whether the CLI docs are richer than what we've surfaced in the agent instructions.

message.content: Yes, let's look at the full help output. It feels like report is an optional workflow outside of the core devdash workflow that allows an agent to report granular status, but this is only useful if a client wants it

content.content:
# Progress Reporting

## Why Report
  Reporting prevents issues from appearing stale (30+ min without activity).
  It's fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.

## Status Values
  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
  committed       Git commit created
  pushed          Pushed to remote
  error           Something went wrong

## When to Report
  After completing code: devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
  After commit:          devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
  After push:            devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
  On error:              devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"

## Always Report Before Exiting
  Even if the work is incomplete, report the current status so the issue
  doesn't appear abandoned.

# Progress Reporting

## Status Values
  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
  committed       Git commit created
  pushed          Pushed to remote
  error           Something went wrong

## When to Report
  After completing code: devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
  After commit:          devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
  After push:            devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
  On error:              devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"

## Always Report Before Exiting
  Even if the work is incomplete, report the current status so the issue
  doesn't appear abandoned.

content.text:
Yeah, your instinct is right. The docs frame it as a staleness-prevention mechanism — it keeps issues from looking abandoned when a long-running agent is quietly working. The status values (`code_complete`, `committed`, `pushed`, `error`) are essentially breadcrumbs between `update --status=in_progress` and `close`.

This is really only useful if something is *watching* — a dashboard, a polling client, or another agent waiting on a dependency. For a single-user workflow where you're already in the conversation, it's noise.

Want me to make the `CLAUDE.md` instructions clearer that `report` is optional/situational rather than part of the core flow? Something like moving it into its own section with a note that it's for long-running or monitored workflows.

message.content: I wonder if we should hide it behind a flag, like "enable enhanced logging" which could provide more detailed agent instructions in what's written to the agent where the CLI is installed.

I wonder if we should hide it behind a flag, like "enable enhanced logging" which could provide more detailed agent instructions in what's written to the agent where the CLI is installed. or maybe just leave the status flags as available in the backend, but unused by the default workflow. Then clients could choose to report on those if needed, and could craft agent instructions and UI to surface those statuses

content.text:
I think the second option is the better design. A few reasons:

**The backend already supports it** — the API accepts report statuses, `devdash report` works. There's nothing to build there.

**The cost is in the agent instructions, not the code.** Right now `report` takes up space in `CLAUDE.md` and `prime` output, which is token budget spent teaching every agent a workflow most won't need. The real lever is whether `prime` emits those instructions or not.

**A flag like `--enhanced-reporting` on `devdash init` or a project config** could control whether `prime` includes the reporting cadence instructions. Default off — the core workflow is just `create → update → close`. Clients that want granular status (a dashboard polling for progress, a CI bot, a multi-agent orchestrator) opt in, and `prime` starts emitting the full reporting instructions.

This keeps the CLI simple:
- `devdash report` always works (it's just an API call)
- The *default agent instructions* don't mention it
- Clients that care add a config flag, which makes `prime` teach the agent to report
- Those same clients build UI to surface the statuses

The alternative (a CLI flag that unlocks the command itself) feels like unnecessary gatekeeping when the real issue is just "should the agent be told to do this."

Want me to create an issue for this?

message.content: yeah, I think this is the right strategy. Let's pull it out of the default agent instructions, and then enhance the help content to make it clear how report and these statuses could be used

input.command: devdash create --title="Move report out of default workflow, enhance help docs" --description="Remove report from default agent instructions in CLAUDE.md. Keep the command available but position it as opt-in for clients that want granular progress tracking. Enhance help content to explain when/why to use report." 2>&1

content.text:
Excellent! Now let me verify the structure and create a comprehensive summary:

Based on my thorough exploration of the repository, here are all the files related to the "report" command, help content system, "prime" command, and other key files:

## Summary of Findings

### 1. **Report Command Implementation**
- **File**: `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/internal/commands/report.go`
- **Description**: Implements the `report` subcommand (lines 11-77). This command allows agents/developers to report progress on an issue at key milestones with statuses like `code_complete`, `committed`, `pushed`, or `error`. It accepts optional flags for summary, files-changed, branch, commit SHA, and error messages. The command makes a POST request to `/beads/{uuid}/report` endpoint.

### 2. **Help Topics System**
- **File**: `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/internal/commands/help_topics.go`
- **Description**: Implements the custom help command system (lines 9-32) and defines all help topics as a Go map (lines 35-200). Contains embedded help text for six topics:
  - `cli` — Full command reference with flags, ID formats, and --since syntax
  - `workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
  - `close` — Close summary expectations with examples
  - `pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
  - `projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work
  - `report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values (lines 179-199)
  
  The help system supports `devdash help <topic>` syntax and falls back to command help if a topic isn't found.

### 3. **Prime Command Implementation**
- **File**: `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/internal/commands/prime.go`
- **Description**: Implements the `prime` subcommand (lines 12-158). This command outputs AI-optimized workflow context for agent injection, including:
  - Current project info and GitHub repo
  - Team roster (limited to 20 members)
  - All known projects with their repos
  - Health stats (open/in-progress/blocked counts)
  - Mandatory rules for issue tracking and commit workflows
  - Quick reference for common commands
  - Output format guidance
  - On-demand reference to help topics (including `devdash help report` at line 153)

### 4. **Root Command & Command Tree**
- **File**: `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/internal/commands/root.go`
- **Description**: Constructs the full command tree. The `NewRootCmd()` function (lines 48-128) registers all subcommands including `newReportCmd(deps)` at line 94 and `newPrimeCmd(deps)` at line 110. Also calls `registerHelpTopics()` at line 125 to set up the custom help system.

### 5. **Agent Instructions & Core Documentation**
- **File**: `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/CLAUDE.md`
- **Description**: The root-level markdown file (lines 1-67) containing agent-specific instructions for using devdash. It includes:
  - Core principles (issue-first, one issue per logical unit, capture reflex)
  - Rules for workflow (create before work, mark in-progress, close after push, etc.)
  - Completing work workflow (git add → commit → push → devdash close)
  - On-demand reference links pointing to help topics including `devdash help report`
  - Agent-specific guidance on when to use devdash vs. built-in task tools

### 6. **Related Documentation Files**
- `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/README.md` — General project documentation
- `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/GETTING-STARTED.md` — Getting started guide
- `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/devdash-cli-go/UNDERSTANDING-THE-WORKFLOW.md` — Detailed workflow documentation

## Key Relationships

The help system is centralized in `help_topics.go` as a `map[string]string` containing all help text. The `report` topic (lines 179-199) provides guidance on progress reporting with status values and usage examples. Both the `prime` command and CLAUDE.md reference this help system with `devdash help report` as a way for agents to access detailed reporting guidance on-demand.

1	package commands
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5	
6		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
7	)
8	
9	func registerHelpTopics(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {
10		rootCmd.SetHelpCommand(&cobra.Command{
11			Use:   "help [topic]",
12			Short: "Help about devdash or a specific topic",
13			Long:  "Available topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report",
14			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
15				if len(args) == 0 {
16					return rootCmd.Help()
17				}
18	
19				if text, ok := helpTopics[args[0]]; ok {
20					fmt.Println(text)
21					return nil
22				}
23	
24				target, _, err := rootCmd.Find(args)
25				if err == nil && target != nil {
26					return target.Help()
27				}
28	
29				fmt.Printf("Unknown help topic: %s\n\nAvailable topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report\n", args[0])
30				return nil
31			},
32		})
33	}

87	
88	  # Batch close multiple issues
89	  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"
90	
91	  # Create a child issue under a parent
92	  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123
93	
94	  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
95	  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,
96	
97		"workflow": `# DevDash Workflow Guide
98	
99	## Starting Work
100	  devdash ready                             See what's available
101	  devdash show <id>                         Read the full issue
102	  devdash update <id> --status=in_progress  Mark as started
103	
104	## Completing Work
105	  git add <files>
106	  git commit -m "message"
107	  git push
108	  devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
109	
110	  Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never close before push.
111	
112	## When to Create Issues
113	  - Before writing ANY code (issue-first rule)
114	  - One issue per commit
115	  - When scope expands mid-task, create a new issue
116	  - For multi-step plans: one parent + child issues per step
117	  - Even spikes/investigations deserve an issue
118	
119	## Decomposition Patterns
120	  - Parent/child: "parts of a whole" (--parent=<id>)
121	  - Dependencies: "X must happen before Y" (dep add)
122	  - Prefer parent/child for breakdown, dependencies for ordering
123	
124	## Before Starting Work
125	  Always run devdash show <id> and check:
126	  - parentBeadId: understand the larger goal
127	  - blockedBy/blocks: understand ordering constraints
128	  - preInstructions: agent-specific context`,
129	
130		"close": `# Close Summary Guide
131	
132	Close summaries are institutional memory. Future agents and humans will
133	read them to understand what happened.
134	
135	## What to Include
136	  - What changed (files, functions, behavior)
137	  - Why (the motivation, not just "fixed the bug")
138	  - Decisions made and alternatives considered
139	  - Surprises or gotchas discovered
140	  - Follow-up work needed
141	
142	## Examples
143	
144	  Good: "Added cursor-based pagination to FetchAll. Chose generic approach
145	  with type parameter to avoid duplication. API returns plain arrays for
146	  some endpoints — added fallback unmarshaling."
147	
148	  Bad: "Done"
149	  Bad: "Fixed the issue"
150	  Bad: "Implemented as described"
151	
152	## Metadata
153	  --summary="..."        What changed and why
154	  --commit=SHA           Git commit SHA
155	  --pr=URL               Pull request URL (if applicable)`,
156	
157		"pr": "# Pull Request Format\n\n## DevDash Footer\nEvery PR should include a DevDash footer section:\n\n  ## DevDash\n  Project: `95ca3de0-7e4f-4f9e-9b17-36f5609cfa11`\n  Issues:\n  - [<issue-id>](https://dev-dash-blue.vercel.app/issue/<issue-id>)\n\nReplace <issue-id> with the full UUID of each devdash issue.\nIf the PR addresses multiple issues, list each on its own line.",
158	
159		"projects": `# Cross-Project Work
160	
161	## Targeting a Different Project
162	  DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>
163	
164	## Finding Beads Across Projects
165	  devdash find <full-uuid>
166	
167	  Returns bead with project context (projectId, projectName).
168	  Requires full UUID — prefix lookup only works within a project.
169	
170	## Cross-Project Dependencies
171	  Dependencies work across projects. The blocker bead is resolved globally
172	  (not project-scoped), so you can link issues from different projects.
173	
174	  Use full UUIDs for cross-project bead references:
175	    devdash dep add <issue-uuid> <blocker-uuid-from-other-project>
176	
177	  The server verifies you have access to both projects.`,
178	
179		"report": `# Progress Reporting
180	
181	## Why Report
182	  Reporting prevents issues from appearing stale (30+ min without activity).
183	  It's fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.
184	
185	## Status Values
186	  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
187	  committed       Git commit created
188	  pushed          Pushed to remote
189	  error           Something went wrong
190	
191	## When to Report
192	  After completing code: devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
193	  After commit:          devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
194	  After push:            devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
195	  On error:              devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"
196	
197	## Always Report Before Exiting
198	  Even if the work is incomplete, report the current status so the issue
199	  doesn't appear abandoned.`,
200	}
201

package commands

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func registerHelpTopics(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {
	rootCmd.SetHelpCommand(&cobra.Command{
		Use:   "help [topic]",
		Short: "Help about devdash or a specific topic",
		Long:  "Available topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report",
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			if len(args) == 0 {
				return rootCmd.Help()
			}

			if text, ok := helpTopics[args[0]]; ok {
				fmt.Println(text)
				return nil
			}

			target, _, err := rootCmd.Find(args)
			if err == nil && target != nil {
				return target.Help()
			}

			fmt.Printf("Unknown help topic: %s\n\nAvailable topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report\n", args[0])
			return nil
		},
	})
}

var helpTopics = map[string]string{
	"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference

## Commands
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  version                              Print version

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,

	"workflow": `# DevDash Workflow Guide

## Starting Work
  devdash ready                             See what's available
  devdash show <id>                         Read the full issue
  devdash update <id> --status=in_progress  Mark as started

## Completing Work
  git add <files>
  git commit -m "message"
  git push
  devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never close before push.

## When to Create Issues
  - Before writing ANY code (issue-first rule)
  - One issue per commit
  - When scope expands mid-task, create a new issue
  - For multi-step plans: one parent + child issues per step
  - Even spikes/investigations deserve an issue

## Decomposition Patterns
  - Parent/child: "parts of a whole" (--parent=<id>)
  - Dependencies: "X must happen before Y" (dep add)
  - Prefer parent/child for breakdown, dependencies for ordering

## Before Starting Work
  Always run devdash show <id> and check:
  - parentBeadId: understand the larger goal
  - blockedBy/blocks: understand ordering constraints
  - preInstructions: agent-specific context`,

	"close": `# Close Summary Guide

Close summaries are institutional memory. Future agents and humans will
read them to understand what happened.

## What to Include
  - What changed (files, functions, behavior)
  - Why (the motivation, not just "fixed the bug")
  - Decisions made and alternatives considered
  - Surprises or gotchas discovered
  - Follow-up work needed

## Examples

  Good: "Added cursor-based pagination to FetchAll. Chose generic approach
  with type parameter to avoid duplication. API returns plain arrays for
  some endpoints — added fallback unmarshaling."

  Bad: "Done"
  Bad: "Fixed the issue"
  Bad: "Implemented as described"

## Metadata
  --summary="..."        What changed and why
  --commit=SHA           Git commit SHA
  --pr=URL               Pull request URL (if applicable)`,

	"pr": "# Pull Request Format\n\n## DevDash Footer\nEvery PR should include a DevDash footer section:\n\n  ## DevDash\n  Project: `95ca3de0-7e4f-4f9e-9b17-36f5609cfa11`\n  Issues:\n  - [<issue-id>](https://dev-dash-blue.vercel.app/issue/<issue-id>)\n\nReplace <issue-id> with the full UUID of each devdash issue.\nIf the PR addresses multiple issues, list each on its own line.",

	"projects": `# Cross-Project Work

## Targeting a Different Project
  DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>

## Finding Beads Across Projects
  devdash find <full-uuid>

  Returns bead with project context (projectId, projectName).
  Requires full UUID — prefix lookup only works within a project.

## Cross-Project Dependencies
  Dependencies work across projects. The blocker bead is resolved globally
  (not project-scoped), so you can link issues from different projects.

  Use full UUIDs for cross-project bead references:
    devdash dep add <issue-uuid> <blocker-uuid-from-other-project>

  The server verifies you have access to both projects.`,

	"report": `# Progress Reporting

## Why Report
  Reporting prevents issues from appearing stale (30+ min without activity).
  It's fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.

## Status Values
  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
  committed       Git commit created
  pushed          Pushed to remote
  error           Something went wrong

## When to Report
  After completing code: devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
  After commit:          devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
  After push:            devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
  On error:              devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"

## Always Report Before Exiting
  Even if the work is incomplete, report the current status so the issue
  doesn't appear abandoned.`,
}

1	package commands
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7	
8		"github.com/jasonmassey/devdash-cli-go/internal/api"
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	)
11	
12	func newPrimeCmd(d *Deps) *cobra.Command {
13		return &cobra.Command{
14			Use:   "prime",
15			Short: "Output AI-optimized workflow context for agent injection",
16			Long: `Output a full AI-optimized workflow context block designed for agent injection.
17	
18	The output includes the current project info, team roster, all known projects,
19	health stats (open/in-progress/blocked counts), mandatory rules, a quick
20	reference for common workflows, and on-demand help topics.
21	
22	Run this at the start of every new session, after context compaction, or
23	whenever the agent has lost track of project state. The output is formatted
24	as Markdown so it can be injected directly into an agent's context window.`,
25			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
26				pid, err := d.requireProject(cmd)
27				if err != nil {
28					return err
29				}
30	
31				projData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid)
32				var project api.Project
33				_ = json.Unmarshal(projData, &project)
34	
35				allProjData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects")
36				var allProjects []api.Project
37				_ = json.Unmarshal(allProjData, &allProjects)
38	
39				beads, _ := api.FetchAll[api.Bead](d.Client, "/beads?projectId="+pid)
40	
41				teamData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid + "/members?format=compact")
42				var members []api.TeamMember
43				_ = json.Unmarshal(teamData, &members)
44	
45				var open, inProgress, blocked int
46				completedIDs := make(map[string]bool)
47				for _, b := range beads {
48					switch b.Status {
49					case "pending":
50						open++
51					case "in_progress":
52						inProgress++
53					case "completed":
54						completedIDs[b.ID] = true
55					}
56				}
57				for _, b := range beads {
58					if b.Status == "pending" && len(b.BlockedBy) > 0 && isBlocked(b, completedIDs) {
59						blocked++
60					}
61				}
62	
63				fmt.Println("# Dev-Dash Workflow Context")
64				fmt.Println()
65				fmt.Println("> **Context Recovery**: Run `devdash prime` after compaction, clear, or new session.")
66				fmt.Println("> Use `devdash` (dev-dash CLI) for ALL task tracking — never `bd`.")
67				fmt.Println()
68	
69				repo := ""
70				if project.GithubRepo != "" {
71					repo = fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", project.GithubRepo)
72				}
73				fmt.Printf("**Project**: %s%s  |  **ID**: `%s`\n", project.Name, repo, shortID(project.ID))
74				fmt.Printf("**Health**: %d open, %d in progress, %d blocked\n", open, inProgress, blocked)
75				fmt.Println()
76	
77				if len(members) > 0 {
78					fmt.Println("## Team")
79					fmt.Println()
80					fmt.Println("| Name | Username | Email | Role |")
81					fmt.Println("|------|----------|-------|------|")
82					cap := 20
83					if len(members) < cap {
84						cap = len(members)
85					}
86					for _, m := range members[:cap] {
87						name := m.Name
88						if name == "" {
89							name = m.Email
90						}
91						username := m.Username
92						if username != "" {
93							username = "@" + username
94						}
95						fmt.Printf("| %s | %s | %s | %s |\n", name, username, m.Email, m.Role)
96					}
97					fmt.Println()
98					fmt.Println("Assign with: `devdash update <id> --owner=<email-or-name>`")
99					fmt.Println()
100				}
101	
102				fmt.Println("## All Projects")
103				fmt.Println()
104				fmt.Println("| Short | Full UUID | Name | Repo |")
105				fmt.Println("|-------|-----------|------|------|")
106				for _, p := range allProjects {
107					r := "(no repo)"
108					if p.GithubRepo != "" {
109						r = fmt.Sprintf("(%s)", p.GithubRepo)
110					}
111					fmt.Printf("| `%s` | `%s` | %s | %s |\n", shortID(p.ID), p.ID, p.Name, r)
112				}
113				fmt.Println()
114				fmt.Println("Use `DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>` to target a specific project.")
115				fmt.Println("Short prefixes work for project IDs too: `DD_PROJECT_ID=47eb046a devdash ready`")
116				fmt.Println()
117	
118				fmt.Println("## Rules (MANDATORY)")
119				fmt.Println("- **Issue-first**: Create a devdash issue BEFORE writing code. No exceptions.")
120				fmt.Println("- **Issue-per-commit**: Every git commit must map to a devdash issue. If scope expands, create new issues.")
121				fmt.Println("- **Mark in-progress**: `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work.")
122				fmt.Println("- **Pre-commit checkpoint**: Before each `git commit`, verify you have an issue. If not, create one.")
123				fmt.Println("- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.")
124				fmt.Println("- **No orphaned work**: At session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.")
125				exe, _ := os.Executable()
126				fmt.Printf("- **Path**: `%s`\n", exe)
127				fmt.Println("- **Preserve stderr**: Avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands — stderr contains error details you'll need for debugging failures.")
128				fmt.Println("- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use `bd`, TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking")
129				fmt.Println()
130	
131				fmt.Println("## Quick Reference")
132				fmt.Println("- **Start (task already named)**: `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
133				fmt.Println("- **Start (need a task)**: `devdash ready` → `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
134				fmt.Println("- **Complete**: git add → commit → push → `devdash close <id> --summary=\"...\" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`")
135				fmt.Println("- **Report**: `devdash report <id> --status=code_complete|committed|pushed|error --summary=\"...\"`")
136				fmt.Println("- Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.")
137				fmt.Println("- One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.")
138				fmt.Println()
139	
140				fmt.Println("## Output Formats")
141				fmt.Println("- There is no `--json` flag. Do not try `--json`, `--format`, or `--output`.")
142				fmt.Println("- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`")
143				fmt.Println("- All other commands return human-readable text. Parse UUIDs from `create`/`close`/`update` output directly.")
144				fmt.Println()
145	
146				fmt.Println("## On-Demand Reference")
147				fmt.Println("Run these when you need detailed guidance:")
148				fmt.Println("- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)")
149				fmt.Println("- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships")
150				fmt.Println("- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples")
151				fmt.Println("- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs")
152				fmt.Println("- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work")
153				fmt.Println("- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values")
154	
155				return nil
156			},
157		}
158	}
159

package commands

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/jasonmassey/devdash-cli-go/internal/api"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func newPrimeCmd(d *Deps) *cobra.Command {
	return &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "prime",
		Short: "Output AI-optimized workflow context for agent injection",
		Long: `Output a full AI-optimized workflow context block designed for agent injection.

The output includes the current project info, team roster, all known projects,
health stats (open/in-progress/blocked counts), mandatory rules, a quick
reference for common workflows, and on-demand help topics.

Run this at the start of every new session, after context compaction, or
whenever the agent has lost track of project state. The output is formatted
as Markdown so it can be injected directly into an agent's context window.`,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			pid, err := d.requireProject(cmd)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}

			projData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid)
			var project api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(projData, &project)

			allProjData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects")
			var allProjects []api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(allProjData, &allProjects)

			beads, _ := api.FetchAll[api.Bead](d.Client, "/beads?projectId="+pid)

			teamData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid + "/members?format=compact")
			var members []api.TeamMember
			_ = json.Unmarshal(teamData, &members)

			var open, inProgress, blocked int
			completedIDs := make(map[string]bool)
			for _, b := range beads {
				switch b.Status {
				case "pending":
					open++
				case "in_progress":
					inProgress++
				case "completed":
					completedIDs[b.ID] = true
				}
			}
			for _, b := range beads {
				if b.Status == "pending" && len(b.BlockedBy) > 0 && isBlocked(b, completedIDs) {
					blocked++
				}
			}

			fmt.Println("# Dev-Dash Workflow Context")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("> **Context Recovery**: Run `devdash prime` after compaction, clear, or new session.")
			fmt.Println("> Use `devdash` (dev-dash CLI) for ALL task tracking — never `bd`.")
			fmt.Println()

			repo := ""
			if project.GithubRepo != "" {
				repo = fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", project.GithubRepo)
			}
			fmt.Printf("**Project**: %s%s  |  **ID**: `%s`\n", project.Name, repo, shortID(project.ID))
			fmt.Printf("**Health**: %d open, %d in progress, %d blocked\n", open, inProgress, blocked)
			fmt.Println()

			if len(members) > 0 {
				fmt.Println("## Team")
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("| Name | Username | Email | Role |")
				fmt.Println("|------|----------|-------|------|")
				cap := 20
				if len(members) < cap {
					cap = len(members)
				}
				for _, m := range members[:cap] {
					name := m.Name
					if name == "" {
						name = m.Email
					}
					username := m.Username
					if username != "" {
						username = "@" + username
					}
					fmt.Printf("| %s | %s | %s | %s |\n", name, username, m.Email, m.Role)
				}
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("Assign with: `devdash update <id> --owner=<email-or-name>`")
				fmt.Println()
			}

			fmt.Println("## All Projects")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("| Short | Full UUID | Name | Repo |")
			fmt.Println("|-------|-----------|------|------|")
			for _, p := range allProjects {
				r := "(no repo)"
				if p.GithubRepo != "" {
					r = fmt.Sprintf("(%s)", p.GithubRepo)
				}
				fmt.Printf("| `%s` | `%s` | %s | %s |\n", shortID(p.ID), p.ID, p.Name, r)
			}
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("Use `DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>` to target a specific project.")
			fmt.Println("Short prefixes work for project IDs too: `DD_PROJECT_ID=47eb046a devdash ready`")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Rules (MANDATORY)")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-first**: Create a devdash issue BEFORE writing code. No exceptions.")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-per-commit**: Every git commit must map to a devdash issue. If scope expands, create new issues.")
			fmt.Println("- **Mark in-progress**: `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work.")
			fmt.Println("- **Pre-commit checkpoint**: Before each `git commit`, verify you have an issue. If not, create one.")
			fmt.Println("- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.")
			fmt.Println("- **No orphaned work**: At session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.")
			exe, _ := os.Executable()
			fmt.Printf("- **Path**: `%s`\n", exe)
			fmt.Println("- **Preserve stderr**: Avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands — stderr contains error details you'll need for debugging failures.")
			fmt.Println("- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use `bd`, TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Quick Reference")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (task already named)**: `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (need a task)**: `devdash ready` → `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Complete**: git add → commit → push → `devdash close <id> --summary=\"...\" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`")
			fmt.Println("- **Report**: `devdash report <id> --status=code_complete|committed|pushed|error --summary=\"...\"`")
			fmt.Println("- Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.")
			fmt.Println("- One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Output Formats")
			fmt.Println("- There is no `--json` flag. Do not try `--json`, `--format`, or `--output`.")
			fmt.Println("- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`")
			fmt.Println("- All other commands return human-readable text. Parse UUIDs from `create`/`close`/`update` output directly.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## On-Demand Reference")
			fmt.Println("Run these when you need detailed guidance:")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values")

			return nil
		},
	}
}

<!-- devdash:agent-instructions -->

# DevDash — AI Agent Task Tracking

This project uses **devdash** (`dd`) for task tracking. DevDash is a shared memory
between you and the user — a place where ideas, decisions, and progress are captured so
nothing gets lost.

Run devdash commands yourself via the terminal — do not just tell the user to run them.
Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, `bd`, or markdown files for tracking. When the user
says "dd", they mean the devdash CLI, not the Unix `dd` data-copy utility.

Issues are called "beads" internally. You'll see this in fields like `parentBeadId`.

## Core Principles

**Be a capture reflex.**
When the user mentions a bug, idea, TODO, or "we should probably..." — offer to create
an issue. Don't wait to be asked.

**Issue-first.**
Create an issue before doing work. Your first action when asked to implement something
should be `devdash create`.

**One issue per logical unit of work.**
If a task has multiple steps, create a parent issue and child issues. Scope creep during
a task = new issue, not an expanded current one. Every git commit must map to a devdash
issue.

## Rules

1. Create an issue before starting work. No exceptions.
2. `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work on an issue.
3. Close with a substantive summary — write it for a future reader with zero context.
4. Don't batch unrelated work into a single issue.
- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.
6. No orphaned work: at session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.
7. Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never run git and devdash close in parallel.
8. Preserve stderr: avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands.

## Completing Work

`git add` → `git commit` → `git push` → `devdash close <id>`
On successful completions: `devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`.
If a PR exists, include `--pr=URL` too.
Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.
One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.
Report at milestones: `devdash report <id> --status=code_complete|committed|pushed|error --summary="..."`

## On-Demand Reference

Run these when you need detailed guidance:
- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)
- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples
- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work
- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values

## Agent-Specific Instructions

- You may use your built-in task tools (TaskCreate, TodoWrite, etc.) for your own tracking, but you **must also** create and update devdash issues. Devdash is the system of record.
- When the user asks you to implement a plan, feature, or fix: your **very first action** is `devdash create`. Do not read files, do not write code — create the issue first.
- For multi-step plans, create one devdash issue per step before starting any implementation. Group them under a parent issue. Then work through them sequentially: mark in-progress, implement, commit, close, move to next.
- After creating issues, follow the normal workflow: mark in-progress, do the work, commit, then close.
<!-- /devdash:agent-instructions -->

package commands

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/jasonmassey/devdash-cli-go/internal/api"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func newPrimeCmd(d *Deps) *cobra.Command {
	return &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "prime",
		Short: "Output AI-optimized workflow context for agent injection",
		Long: `Output a full AI-optimized workflow context block designed for agent injection.

The output includes the current project info, team roster, all known projects,
health stats (open/in-progress/blocked counts), mandatory rules, a quick
reference for common workflows, and on-demand help topics.

Run this at the start of every new session, after context compaction, or
whenever the agent has lost track of project state. The output is formatted
as Markdown so it can be injected directly into an agent's context window.`,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			pid, err := d.requireProject(cmd)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}

			projData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid)
			var project api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(projData, &project)

			allProjData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects")
			var allProjects []api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(allProjData, &allProjects)

			beads, _ := api.FetchAll[api.Bead](d.Client, "/beads?projectId="+pid)

			teamData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid + "/members?format=compact")
			var members []api.TeamMember
			_ = json.Unmarshal(teamData, &members)

			var open, inProgress, blocked int
			completedIDs := make(map[string]bool)
			for _, b := range beads {
				switch b.Status {
				case "pending":
					open++
				case "in_progress":
					inProgress++
				case "completed":
					completedIDs[b.ID] = true
				}
			}
			for _, b := range beads {
				if b.Status == "pending" && len(b.BlockedBy) > 0 && isBlocked(b, completedIDs) {
					blocked++
				}
			}

			fmt.Println("# Dev-Dash Workflow Context")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("> **Context Recovery**: Run `devdash prime` after compaction, clear, or new session.")
			fmt.Println("> Use `devdash` (dev-dash CLI) for ALL task tracking — never `bd`.")
			fmt.Println()

			repo := ""
			if project.GithubRepo != "" {
				repo = fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", project.GithubRepo)
			}
			fmt.Printf("**Project**: %s%s  |  **ID**: `%s`\n", project.Name, repo, shortID(project.ID))
			fmt.Printf("**Health**: %d open, %d in progress, %d blocked\n", open, inProgress, blocked)
			fmt.Println()

			if len(members) > 0 {
				fmt.Println("## Team")
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("| Name | Username | Email | Role |")
				fmt.Println("|------|----------|-------|------|")
				cap := 20
				if len(members) < cap {
					cap = len(members)
				}
				for _, m := range members[:cap] {
					name := m.Name
					if name == "" {
						name = m.Email
					}
					username := m.Username
					if username != "" {
						username = "@" + username
					}
					fmt.Printf("| %s | %s | %s | %s |\n", name, username, m.Email, m.Role)
				}
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("Assign with: `devdash update <id> --owner=<email-or-name>`")
				fmt.Println()
			}

			fmt.Println("## All Projects")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("| Short | Full UUID | Name | Repo |")
			fmt.Println("|-------|-----------|------|------|")
			for _, p := range allProjects {
				r := "(no repo)"
				if p.GithubRepo != "" {
					r = fmt.Sprintf("(%s)", p.GithubRepo)
				}
				fmt.Printf("| `%s` | `%s` | %s | %s |\n", shortID(p.ID), p.ID, p.Name, r)
			}
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("Use `DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>` to target a specific project.")
			fmt.Println("Short prefixes work for project IDs too: `DD_PROJECT_ID=47eb046a devdash ready`")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Rules (MANDATORY)")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-first**: Create a devdash issue BEFORE writing code. No exceptions.")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-per-commit**: Every git commit must map to a devdash issue. If scope expands, create new issues.")
			fmt.Println("- **Mark in-progress**: `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work.")
			fmt.Println("- **Pre-commit checkpoint**: Before each `git commit`, verify you have an issue. If not, create one.")
			fmt.Println("- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.")
			fmt.Println("- **No orphaned work**: At session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.")
			exe, _ := os.Executable()
			fmt.Printf("- **Path**: `%s`\n", exe)
			fmt.Println("- **Preserve stderr**: Avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands — stderr contains error details you'll need for debugging failures.")
			fmt.Println("- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use `bd`, TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Quick Reference")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (task already named)**: `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (need a task)**: `devdash ready` → `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Complete**: git add → commit → push → `devdash close <id> --summary=\"...\" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`")
			fmt.Println("- **Report**: `devdash report <id> --status=code_complete|committed|pushed|error --summary=\"...\"`")
			fmt.Println("- Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.")
			fmt.Println("- One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Output Formats")
			fmt.Println("- There is no `--json` flag. Do not try `--json`, `--format`, or `--output`.")
			fmt.Println("- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`")
			fmt.Println("- All other commands return human-readable text. Parse UUIDs from `create`/`close`/`update` output directly.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## On-Demand Reference")
			fmt.Println("Run these when you need detailed guidance:")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values")

			return nil
		},
	}
}

package commands

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/jasonmassey/devdash-cli-go/internal/api"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func newPrimeCmd(d *Deps) *cobra.Command {
	return &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "prime",
		Short: "Output AI-optimized workflow context for agent injection",
		Long: `Output a full AI-optimized workflow context block designed for agent injection.

The output includes the current project info, team roster, all known projects,
health stats (open/in-progress/blocked counts), mandatory rules, a quick
reference for common workflows, and on-demand help topics.

Run this at the start of every new session, after context compaction, or
whenever the agent has lost track of project state. The output is formatted
as Markdown so it can be injected directly into an agent's context window.`,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			pid, err := d.requireProject(cmd)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}

			projData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid)
			var project api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(projData, &project)

			allProjData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects")
			var allProjects []api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(allProjData, &allProjects)

			beads, _ := api.FetchAll[api.Bead](d.Client, "/beads?projectId="+pid)

			teamData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid + "/members?format=compact")
			var members []api.TeamMember
			_ = json.Unmarshal(teamData, &members)

			var open, inProgress, blocked int
			completedIDs := make(map[string]bool)
			for _, b := range beads {
				switch b.Status {
				case "pending":
					open++
				case "in_progress":
					inProgress++
				case "completed":
					completedIDs[b.ID] = true
				}
			}
			for _, b := range beads {
				if b.Status == "pending" && len(b.BlockedBy) > 0 && isBlocked(b, completedIDs) {
					blocked++
				}
			}

			fmt.Println("# Dev-Dash Workflow Context")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("> **Context Recovery**: Run `devdash prime` after compaction, clear, or new session.")
			fmt.Println("> Use `devdash` (dev-dash CLI) for ALL task tracking — never `bd`.")
			fmt.Println()

			repo := ""
			if project.GithubRepo != "" {
				repo = fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", project.GithubRepo)
			}
			fmt.Printf("**Project**: %s%s  |  **ID**: `%s`\n", project.Name, repo, shortID(project.ID))
			fmt.Printf("**Health**: %d open, %d in progress, %d blocked\n", open, inProgress, blocked)
			fmt.Println()

			if len(members) > 0 {
				fmt.Println("## Team")
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("| Name | Username | Email | Role |")
				fmt.Println("|------|----------|-------|------|")
				cap := 20
				if len(members) < cap {
					cap = len(members)
				}
				for _, m := range members[:cap] {
					name := m.Name
					if name == "" {
						name = m.Email
					}
					username := m.Username
					if username != "" {
						username = "@" + username
					}
					fmt.Printf("| %s | %s | %s | %s |\n", name, username, m.Email, m.Role)
				}
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("Assign with: `devdash update <id> --owner=<email-or-name>`")
				fmt.Println()
			}

			fmt.Println("## All Projects")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("| Short | Full UUID | Name | Repo |")
			fmt.Println("|-------|-----------|------|------|")
			for _, p := range allProjects {
				r := "(no repo)"
				if p.GithubRepo != "" {
					r = fmt.Sprintf("(%s)", p.GithubRepo)
				}
				fmt.Printf("| `%s` | `%s` | %s | %s |\n", shortID(p.ID), p.ID, p.Name, r)
			}
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("Use `DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>` to target a specific project.")
			fmt.Println("Short prefixes work for project IDs too: `DD_PROJECT_ID=47eb046a devdash ready`")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Rules (MANDATORY)")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-first**: Create a devdash issue BEFORE writing code. No exceptions.")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-per-commit**: Every git commit must map to a devdash issue. If scope expands, create new issues.")
			fmt.Println("- **Mark in-progress**: `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work.")
			fmt.Println("- **Pre-commit checkpoint**: Before each `git commit`, verify you have an issue. If not, create one.")
			fmt.Println("- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.")
			fmt.Println("- **No orphaned work**: At session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.")
			exe, _ := os.Executable()
			fmt.Printf("- **Path**: `%s`\n", exe)
			fmt.Println("- **Preserve stderr**: Avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands — stderr contains error details you'll need for debugging failures.")
			fmt.Println("- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use `bd`, TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Quick Reference")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (task already named)**: `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (need a task)**: `devdash ready` → `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Complete**: git add → commit → push → `devdash close <id> --summary=\"...\" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`")
			fmt.Println("- Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.")
			fmt.Println("- One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Output Formats")
			fmt.Println("- There is no `--json` flag. Do not try `--json`, `--format`, or `--output`.")
			fmt.Println("- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`")
			fmt.Println("- All other commands return human-readable text. Parse UUIDs from `create`/`close`/`update` output directly.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## On-Demand Reference")
			fmt.Println("Run these when you need detailed guidance:")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values")

			return nil
		},
	}
}

input.old_string:
"report": `# Progress Reporting

## Why Report
  Reporting prevents issues from appearing stale (30+ min without activity).
  It's fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.

## Status Values
  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
  committed       Git commit created
  pushed          Pushed to remote
  error           Something went wrong

## When to Report
  After completing code: devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
  After commit:          devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
  After push:            devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
  On error:              devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"

## Always Report Before Exiting
  Even if the work is incomplete, report the current status so the issue
  doesn't appear abandoned.`,
input.new_string:
"report": `# Progress Reporting (Optional)

The report command is NOT part of the default workflow. The core workflow is:
  create → update --status=in_progress → [work] → close

Report exists for clients that want granular, real-time progress tracking —
dashboards, CI bots, multi-agent orchestrators, or any system polling for
intermediate status between "in progress" and "closed".

## When to Use Report
  - A monitoring dashboard is watching issue progress
  - Multiple agents coordinate and need to signal milestones to each other
  - Long-running tasks where a human or system needs visibility before completion
  - Error reporting when work is abandoned mid-task

  If none of these apply, you don't need report. Just close the issue when done.

## Status Values
  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
  committed       Git commit created
  pushed          Pushed to remote
  error           Something went wrong

## Usage
  devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
  devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
  devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
  devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"

  Report is fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.

## For Client Developers
  Report statuses are available in the API for any client to consume.
  Clients that want agents to report should include reporting instructions
  in their agent context (e.g., via prime output or custom agent prompts).
  The default prime output does not include reporting instructions.`,

package commands

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func registerHelpTopics(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {
	rootCmd.SetHelpCommand(&cobra.Command{
		Use:   "help [topic]",
		Short: "Help about devdash or a specific topic",
		Long:  "Available topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report",
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			if len(args) == 0 {
				return rootCmd.Help()
			}

			if text, ok := helpTopics[args[0]]; ok {
				fmt.Println(text)
				return nil
			}

			target, _, err := rootCmd.Find(args)
			if err == nil && target != nil {
				return target.Help()
			}

			fmt.Printf("Unknown help topic: %s\n\nAvailable topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report\n", args[0])
			return nil
		},
	})
}

var helpTopics = map[string]string{
	"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference

## Commands
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  version                              Print version

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,

	"workflow": `# DevDash Workflow Guide

## Starting Work
  devdash ready                             See what's available
  devdash show <id>                         Read the full issue
  devdash update <id> --status=in_progress  Mark as started

## Completing Work
  git add <files>
  git commit -m "message"
  git push
  devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never close before push.

## When to Create Issues
  - Before writing ANY code (issue-first rule)
  - One issue per commit
  - When scope expands mid-task, create a new issue
  - For multi-step plans: one parent + child issues per step
  - Even spikes/investigations deserve an issue

## Decomposition Patterns
  - Parent/child: "parts of a whole" (--parent=<id>)
  - Dependencies: "X must happen before Y" (dep add)
  - Prefer parent/child for breakdown, dependencies for ordering

## Before Starting Work
  Always run devdash show <id> and check:
  - parentBeadId: understand the larger goal
  - blockedBy/blocks: understand ordering constraints
  - preInstructions: agent-specific context`,

	"close": `# Close Summary Guide

Close summaries are institutional memory. Future agents and humans will
read them to understand what happened.

## What to Include
  - What changed (files, functions, behavior)
  - Why (the motivation, not just "fixed the bug")
  - Decisions made and alternatives considered
  - Surprises or gotchas discovered
  - Follow-up work needed

## Examples

  Good: "Added cursor-based pagination to FetchAll. Chose generic approach
  with type parameter to avoid duplication. API returns plain arrays for
  some endpoints — added fallback unmarshaling."

  Bad: "Done"
  Bad: "Fixed the issue"
  Bad: "Implemented as described"

## Metadata
  --summary="..."        What changed and why
  --commit=SHA           Git commit SHA
  --pr=URL               Pull request URL (if applicable)`,

	"pr": "# Pull Request Format\n\n## DevDash Footer\nEvery PR should include a DevDash footer section:\n\n  ## DevDash\n  Project: `95ca3de0-7e4f-4f9e-9b17-36f5609cfa11`\n  Issues:\n  - [<issue-id>](https://dev-dash-blue.vercel.app/issue/<issue-id>)\n\nReplace <issue-id> with the full UUID of each devdash issue.\nIf the PR addresses multiple issues, list each on its own line.",

	"projects": `# Cross-Project Work

## Targeting a Different Project
  DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>

## Finding Beads Across Projects
  devdash find <full-uuid>

  Returns bead with project context (projectId, projectName).
  Requires full UUID — prefix lookup only works within a project.

## Cross-Project Dependencies
  Dependencies work across projects. The blocker bead is resolved globally
  (not project-scoped), so you can link issues from different projects.

  Use full UUIDs for cross-project bead references:
    devdash dep add <issue-uuid> <blocker-uuid-from-other-project>

  The server verifies you have access to both projects.`,

	"report": `# Progress Reporting

## Why Report
  Reporting prevents issues from appearing stale (30+ min without activity).
  It's fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.

## Status Values
  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
  committed       Git commit created
  pushed          Pushed to remote
  error           Something went wrong

## When to Report
  After completing code: devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
  After commit:          devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
  After push:            devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
  On error:              devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"

## Always Report Before Exiting
  Even if the work is incomplete, report the current status so the issue
  doesn't appear abandoned.`,
}

  git commit -m "message"
  git push
  devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never close before push.

## When to Create Issues
  - Before writing ANY code (issue-first rule)
  - One issue per commit
  - When scope expands mid-task, create a new issue
  - For multi-step plans: one parent + child issues per step
  - Even spikes/investigations deserve an issue

## Decomposition Patterns
  - Parent/child: "parts of a whole" (--parent=<id>)
  - Dependencies: "X must happen before Y" (dep add)
  - Prefer parent/child for breakdown, dependencies for ordering

## Before Starting Work
  Always run devdash show <id> and check:
  - parentBeadId: understand the larger goal
  - blockedBy/blocks: understand ordering constraints
  - preInstructions: agent-specific context`,

	"close": `# Close Summary Guide

Close summaries are institutional memory. Future agents and humans will
read them to understand what happened.

## What to Include
  - What changed (files, functions, behavior)
  - Why (the motivation, not just "fixed the bug")
  - Decisions made and alternatives considered
  - Surprises or gotchas discovered
  - Follow-up work needed

## Examples

  Good: "Added cursor-based pagination to FetchAll. Chose generic approach
  with type parameter to avoid duplication. API returns plain arrays for
  some endpoints — added fallback unmarshaling."

  Bad: "Done"
  Bad: "Fixed the issue"
  Bad: "Implemented as described"

  --summary="..."        What changed and why
  --commit=SHA           Git commit SHA
  --pr=URL               Pull request URL (if applicable)`,

	"projects": `# Cross-Project Work

package commands

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func registerHelpTopics(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {
	rootCmd.SetHelpCommand(&cobra.Command{
		Use:   "help [topic]",
		Short: "Help about devdash or a specific topic",
		Long:  "Available topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report",
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			if len(args) == 0 {
				return rootCmd.Help()
			}

			if text, ok := helpTopics[args[0]]; ok {
				fmt.Println(text)
				return nil
			}

			target, _, err := rootCmd.Find(args)
			if err == nil && target != nil {
				return target.Help()
			}

			fmt.Printf("Unknown help topic: %s\n\nAvailable topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report\n", args[0])
			return nil
		},
	})
}

message.content: aren't "ready" and "blocked" just subsets of "list" ?

content.text: Good question — let me check the implementations.

content.content:
Found 3 files
internal/commands/ready.go
internal/commands/list.go
internal/commands/blocked.go

content.text:
Yes and no. They all fetch the same `/beads?projectId=` endpoint and filter client-side, but there are meaningful differences:

| | `list` | `ready` | `blocked` |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Statuses shown** | All (or filtered via `--status`) | `pending` only | `pending` only |
| **Dependency check** | None | Excludes blocked | Only blocked |
| **Excludes thoughts** | No | Yes | No |
| **Sort** | Priority | Automability score → priority | Priority |
| **`--since` field** | `updatedAt` | `createdAt` | N/A |
| **`--parent` filter** | Yes | No | No |

So `ready` ≈ `list --status=pending` minus blocked issues minus thoughts, re-sorted by automability. And `blocked` ≈ `list --status=pending` filtered to only those with unsatisfied deps.

They *could* be collapsed into `list` with flags like `--ready` and `--blocked`, but the current design treats them as opinionated views — "what should I work on?" vs "what's stuck?" — which is arguably the right UX for an agent that needs a one-word command to answer a specific question. Thoughts?

message.content: I think it works as designed, let's keep it. Can we compare the CLI surface and the devdash API surface? Also, I think your original list didn't include some commands like "token create"

  version         Print the version

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"

toolUseResult.stdout:
# DevDash CLI Reference

## Commands
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  version                              Print version

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"

input.command: devdash token --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash jobs --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash project --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash admin --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash score --help 2>&1
input.description: Get subcommand help for missing commands

content.content:
Manage API tokens for authenticating with the devdash API.

Subcommands let you create, list, and revoke tokens. Use "token create <name>"
to generate a new named token, "token list" to show all active tokens, and
"token revoke <id>" to revoke one by its ID.

Tokens are scoped to your user account and grant the same access as your
session. Treat them like passwords.

Usage:
  devdash token [command]

Available Commands:
  create      Create a new API token
  list        List API tokens
  revoke      Revoke an API token

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for token

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

Use "devdash token [command] --help" for more information about a command.
---
List recent jobs for the current project, optionally filtered by bead.

By default, prints one summary line per job showing its ID, status, bead,
and creation time. Pass --bead to narrow results to a single issue.

Subcommands provide deeper inspection: "jobs show" dumps full JSON detail
for a single job, "jobs log" streams the output log (with optional --tail),
and "jobs failures" lists the last 10 failed jobs with optional --bead filter.

Usage:
  devdash jobs [flags]
  devdash jobs [command]

Available Commands:
  failures    Recent failed jobs
  log         Job output log
  show        Job details

Flags:
      --bead string   Filter by bead ID
  -h, --help          help for jobs

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

Use "devdash jobs [command] --help" for more information about a command.
---
Parent command for managing DevDash projects.

Subcommands let you create new projects (with --name, --repo, and
--description flags), list all projects you have access to, or delete
a project by ID. Every other devdash command operates within the scope
of a single project, so use this command group to set up and organize
your workspace.

Usage:
  devdash project [command]

Available Commands:
  create      Create a new project
  delete      Delete a project
  list        List projects

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for project

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

Use "devdash project [command] --help" for more information about a command.
---
Admin commands (requires ADMIN_SECRET)

Usage:
  devdash admin [command]

Available Commands:
  reset-user  Reset a user's data

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for admin

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

Use "devdash admin [command] --help" for more information about a command.
---
Score beads to evaluate how suitable they are for automation.

Without an ID, scores every bead in the current project and prints a
table with the automability grade, automability score, and complexity
for each. With an ID, scores a single bead and returns the full JSON
detail including the breakdown factors.

Useful for triaging a backlog to find the best candidates for
automated execution.

Usage:
  devdash score [<id>] [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for score

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)
