input.command: devdash dispatch --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash diagnose --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash analyze --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash sync --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash import --help 2>&1; echo "---"; devdash reconcile-tasks --help 2>&1
input.description: Get help for remaining undocumented commands

content.content:
Queue a bead for execution as a job.

The prompt sent to the worker is chosen from the first non-empty field in this
order: PreInstructions, Description, Subject. Use --priority (0-4) to control
scheduling urgency and --worker to select an execution environment
(docker, e2b, or railway).

Returns the created job ID on success.

Usage:
  devdash dispatch <id> [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help            help for dispatch
      --priority int    Job priority: 0-4 (default 2)
      --worker string   Execution backend: docker, e2b, railway

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)
---
Investigate a bead by showing its current state and associated job history.

Prints a summary line (ID, subject, status, priority, type), then lists all
jobs tied to that bead with their status and creation time. For the first
failed job found, it displays the error message, failure analysis (if any),
and the last 30 lines of the output log.

Useful as a single command to answer "what happened?" when a bead's jobs
aren't completing as expected.

Usage:
  devdash diagnose <id> [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for diagnose

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)
---
Trigger a sandbox analysis job for an issue.

Queues the issue for automated analysis and prints the resulting job ID
to stdout (useful for scripting). The job runs asynchronously — check
its status with "devdash jobs show <job-id>".

Usage:
  devdash analyze <id> [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for analyze

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)
---
Trigger a full GitHub reconciliation for the current project.

Syncs issue state, labels, and metadata between GitHub and devdash
so both systems reflect the same reality. The sync runs server-side
and returns a JSON summary of what changed.

Use this after making changes directly in GitHub (closing issues,
editing labels, updating milestones) to pull those changes back
into devdash.

Usage:
  devdash sync [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for sync

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)
---
Import GitHub issues into devdash as beads.

Pass a single issue number to import one issue, or use --all to bulk-import
every issue from the linked GitHub repository. With --all you can filter by
state (e.g. --state=open).

Returns the imported bead ID for single imports or a count of imported issues
for bulk imports. Requires a project with a linked GitHub repository.

Usage:
  devdash import <issue-number> | --all [flags]

Flags:
      --all            Import all issues
  -h, --help           help for import
      --state string   Issue state filter: open, all (default "open")

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)
---
Audit the project backlog for inconsistencies such as orphaned
dependencies, status mismatches, and unreachable beads.

By default the command runs in --dry-run mode, listing what it found
without changing anything. Pass --auto-fix to apply corrections.
Use --json to get the raw findings as JSON.

Findings are grouped by type and include severity and related bead IDs.

Usage:
  devdash reconcile-tasks [flags]

Flags:
      --auto-fix   Apply fixes automatically
      --dry-run    Preview findings only (default) (default true)
  -h, --help       help for reconcile-tasks
      --json       Output raw JSON

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. Here's the comparison:

## Full CLI Commands (actual) vs `help cli` (documented)

**Missing from `help cli`:**

| CLI Command | What it does | API Endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| `token create <name>` | Create API token | `POST /auth/tokens` |
| `token list` | List API tokens | `GET /auth/tokens` |
| `token revoke <id>` | Revoke a token | `DELETE /auth/tokens/{id}` |
| `jobs [--bead=X]` | List recent jobs | `GET /jobs?projectId=...` |
| `jobs show <id>` | Job details (JSON) | `GET /jobs/{id}` |
| `jobs log <id>` | Job output log | `GET /jobs/{id}` (log field) |
| `jobs failures [--bead=X]` | Recent failed jobs | `GET /jobs?projectId=...` |
| `project create` | Create a project | `POST /projects` |
| `project list` | List projects | `GET /projects` |
| `project delete <id>` | Delete a project | `DELETE /projects/{id}` |
| `dispatch <id>` | Queue bead for execution | `POST /jobs` |
| `diagnose <id>` | Investigate bead + job history | `GET /beads/{id}` + `GET /jobs` |
| `analyze <id>` | Trigger sandbox analysis | `POST /jobs/analyze` |
| `score [<id>]` | Score beads for automability | `POST .../beads/score` |
| `sync` | GitHub reconciliation | `POST /sync/{pid}/sync-all` |
| `import <num> \| --all` | Import GitHub issues | `POST /sync/{pid}/issues/{n}/import` |
| `reconcile-tasks` | Audit backlog inconsistencies | `POST /jobs/reconcile-tasks` |
| `self-update` | Update CLI binary | GitHub releases API |
| `agent-setup` | Configure agent instructions | Local file ops |
| `alias-setup` | Add `dd` shell alias | Local file ops |
| `admin reset-user` | Reset user data | Admin endpoint |

**API Endpoints used by CLI:**

| Endpoint | Used By |
|---|---|
| `GET /projects` | `list`, `prime`, `project list`, `init` |
| `POST /projects` | `project create`, `init` |
| `DELETE /projects/{id}` | `project delete` |
| `GET /projects/{id}/members` | `team`, `prime` |
| `GET /beads?projectId=...` | `list`, `ready`, `blocked`, `stale`, `prime` |
| `GET /beads/{id}` | `show`, `find`, `analyze`, `diagnose`, `dispatch` |
| `POST /beads` | `create` |
| `PATCH /beads/{id}` | `update`, `close` (single) |
| `POST /beads/bulk/close` | `close` (multiple) |
| `DELETE /beads/{id}` | `delete` |
| `POST /beads/{id}/report` | `report` |
| `POST /beads/{id}/dependencies` | `dep add` |
| `DELETE /beads/{id}/dependencies/{dep}` | `dep remove` |
| `POST /beads/{id}/comments` | `comment` |
| `GET /beads/{id}/comments` | `comments` |
| `GET /activity?projectId=...` | `activity` |
| `GET /jobs` | `jobs`, `diagnose` |
| `GET /jobs/{id}` | `jobs show`, `jobs log` |
| `POST /jobs` | `dispatch` |
| `POST /jobs/analyze` | `analyze` |
| `POST /jobs/reconcile-tasks` | `reconcile-tasks` |
| `POST /projects/{id}/beads/score` | `score` (all) |
| `POST /projects/{id}/beads/{id}/score` | `score` (single) |
| `POST /sync/{pid}/sync-all` | `sync` |
| `POST /sync/{pid}/bulk-import` | `import --all` |
| `POST /sync/{pid}/issues/{n}/import` | `import <num>` |
| `POST /auth/tokens` | `token create` |
| `GET /auth/tokens` | `token list` |
| `DELETE /auth/tokens/{id}` | `token revoke` |

The `help cli` topic is significantly out of date — it covers the core workflow commands but is missing the entire `token`, `jobs`, `project`, `score`, `dispatch`, `diagnose`, `analyze`, `sync`, `import`, and `reconcile-tasks` command families. Want me to update it?

message.content: I'm thinking about putting everything job related behind a paywall. How should this affect my API/CLI strategy?

content.text:
This is a good forcing function for how to organize the CLI surface. Based on what I've seen, there's a natural tier boundary:

**Free tier — Issue tracking (the core loop):**
- `create`, `update`, `close`, `delete`
- `list`, `ready`, `blocked`, `stale`, `show`, `find`
- `dep`, `comment`, `comments`, `activity`, `stats`
- `team`, `prime`, `report`
- Setup: `login`, `init`, `doctor`, `version`, `self-update`, `agent-setup`, `alias-setup`

**Paid tier — Execution & automation:**
- `dispatch`, `jobs`, `jobs show`, `jobs log`, `jobs failures`
- `analyze`, `diagnose`, `score`
- `reconcile-tasks`

**Gray area:**
- `sync`, `import` — GitHub integration. Could go either way.
- `token` — Needed for any API integration. Gating this gates all programmatic access.
- `project create/delete/list` — Admin-ish but also basic setup.

This raises a few questions that affect how we update `help cli`:

1. **Should paywalled commands stay in the binary?** I'd say yes — the commands exist but return a clear "this requires plan X" message. Hiding them entirely means users can't discover them.

2. **Should `help cli` document them?** If yes, probably grouped separately so agents don't try to use commands that will fail.

3. **Should `prime` mention paywalled commands?** Probably not — you're spending agent tokens teaching it commands it can't use. Same logic as the `report` change we just made.

4. **Should `score` be paywalled?** It's interesting because `ready` already sorts by automability score. The data exists in the free tier — `score` just exposes the raw breakdown.

sync and import commands should be restructured as provider-based:
`devdash github sync`, `devdash linear sync`, etc.

content.text: Now let me design the implementation plan.

input.prompt:
I need a plan for updating the devdash CLI's `help cli` topic and `prime` command output to:
1. Document ALL commands (many are currently missing from `help cli`)
2. Organize commands by tier (free core, paid execution, WIP integration)
3. Remove paid-tier commands from `prime` output so agents aren't taught commands they can't use

## Context

This is a Go CLI built with cobra. The user has decided:
- **Free tier**: Issue tracking (create, update, close, list, ready, blocked, stale, show, find, delete), deps, comments, activity, stats, team, prime, report, token (create/list/revoke)
- **Paid tier (execution/automation)**: dispatch, jobs (list/show/log/failures), analyze, diagnose, score, reconcile-tasks  
- **Free but WIP**: sync, import (being reworked as provider-based: `devdash github sync` etc.)
- **Setup/utility (free)**: login, init, doctor, version, self-update, agent-setup, alias-setup, project (create/list/delete)
- **Admin**: admin reset-user

We already made a similar change for `report` — removed it from prime and CLAUDE.md, rewrote the help topic to position it as optional.

## Files to modify

### 1. `internal/commands/help_topics.go` (lines 35-200)
Contains the `helpTopics` map. The `"cli"` entry (lines 36-95) is the main target. Currently only documents ~20 commands. Missing: token, jobs, project, dispatch, diagnose, analyze, score, sync, import, reconcile-tasks, self-update, agent-setup, alias-setup, admin.

Also line 13 and line 29 list available topics: "cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report" — these may need updating.

The `"cli"` topic currently has sections: Commands, ID Formats, --since Format, Priority, Examples. It needs reorganization into grouped sections.

### 2. `internal/commands/prime.go` (lines 12-158)
Outputs agent context. Currently has:
- Line 131-137: "Quick Reference" section that includes core workflow commands
- Line 146-152: "On-Demand Reference" that links to help topics

After our earlier change, `report` was already removed from both sections. Now we need to ensure no paid-tier commands leak into prime output. Currently prime doesn't mention dispatch/jobs/analyze/etc anyway, so this may just need a note pointing agents to `help cli` for the full reference.

### 3. `CLAUDE.md` 
After our earlier change, the on-demand reference section already doesn't include report. We should verify it doesn't reference any paid-tier commands either.

## Constraints
- Keep the `help cli` output scannable — agents read this too
- The `report` command was already repositioned as optional in a prior change (same session). Don't undo that work.
- Commands in the CLI binary stay available (no gating logic in this task) — this is purely a documentation/context change
- The help_topics.go `helpTopics` map uses raw string literals for the text content

Please design the specific changes needed for each file, including the new section structure for `help cli`.

content.text:
This is critical. There are two tests at lines 661-673 that assert on specific text in the `help cli` output:

1. **Line 668**: `"choose what to work on next"` -- the current `ready` description says "Use this when you need to choose what to work on next." My proposed new text shortens this, which would break the test.

2. **Line 671**: `"Start here when the user already named the issue"` -- the current `show` description says "Start here when the user already named the issue." My proposed text changes to a shorter description, which would also break the test.

I need to preserve these exact strings in the new `cli` topic text. Let me revise the plan.

---

## Final Implementation Plan

### File 1: `internal/commands/help_topics.go`

**What changes:** Replace the `"cli"` entry value (lines 36-95) with a new tiered version.

**Lines 13 and 29** (topic list strings): No change -- the topic list is already accurate.

**New `"cli"` content** (replacing lines 36-95):

```go
	"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference

## Issue Tracking (Core)
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress (optional, see: help report)

## Setup & Utility
  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  version                              Print version
  self-update                          Update devdash to the latest version
  uninstall [--dry-run]               Remove devdash and its configuration
  agent-setup [--agent=X] [--force]   Configure agent instructions for this repo
  alias-setup                          Add 'dd' alias to your shell RC file

## Project Management
  project create --name="..." [flags] Create a new project (--repo, --description)
  project list                         List all projects
  project delete <project-id>         Delete a project

## Token Management
  token create <name>                  Create a new API token
  token list                           List API tokens
  token revoke <id>                    Revoke an API token

## Execution & Automation (paid plan)
  dispatch <id> [--priority] [--worker]   Queue an issue for execution as a job
  jobs [--bead=X]                         List recent jobs
  jobs show <id>                          Job details (JSON)
  jobs log <id> [--tail=N]               Job output log
  jobs failures [--bead=X]               Recent failed jobs
  analyze <id>                            Trigger sandbox analysis for an issue
  diagnose <id>                           Investigate: status, job history, failures
  score [<id>]                            Score beads for automability
  reconcile-tasks [--auto-fix] [--json]  Audit and fix backlog inconsistencies

## Integration (WIP — being reworked)
  sync                                 Trigger full GitHub reconciliation
  import <issue-number> | --all       Import GitHub issues (--state=open|all)

## Admin
  admin reset-user <user-id>           Reset a user's data (requires ADMIN_SECRET)

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (optional — for monitored workflows)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,
```

**Key decisions preserved from the original:**
- The `ready` description keeps the exact text "Use this when you need to choose what to work on next." (required by test at line 668).
- The `show` description keeps the exact text "Start here when the user already named the issue." (required by test at line 671).
- The Examples section is identical to the current version.
- The `report` line keeps the `(optional, see: help report)` annotation from the earlier session's work.
- ID Formats, --since Format, and Priority sections are unchanged.

**What is new:**
- 6 new sections: "Setup & Utility", "Project Management", "Token Management", "Execution & Automation (paid plan)", "Integration (WIP -- being reworked)", "Admin"
- The old flat "Commands" section is now "Issue Tracking (Core)"
- 15 previously-undocumented commands are now present
- Paid-tier section is clearly labeled with "(paid plan)"
- WIP section explicitly says "being reworked"

---

### File 2: `internal/commands/prime.go`

**Single change on line 141.** Replace:
```go
fmt.Println("- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`")
```

With:
```go
fmt.Println("- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `token list`")
```

This removes three paid/WIP commands from the list: `jobs show` (paid), `score <id>` (paid), `sync` (WIP). The remaining commands (`show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `token list`) are all free-tier.

No other changes to prime.go. The Quick Reference (lines 131-137) and On-Demand Reference (lines 145-151) sections are already clean.

### File 3: `CLAUDE.md`

**New `"cli"` content** (replacing lines 36-95):

```go
	"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference

## Issue Tracking (Core)
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress (optional, see: help report)

## Setup & Utility
  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  version                              Print version
  self-update                          Update devdash to the latest version
  uninstall [--dry-run]               Remove devdash and its configuration
  agent-setup [--agent=X] [--force]   Configure agent instructions for this repo
  alias-setup                          Add 'dd' alias to your shell RC file

## Project Management
  project create --name="..." [flags] Create a new project (--repo, --description)
  project list                         List all projects
  project delete <project-id>         Delete a project

## Token Management
  token create <name>                  Create a new API token
  token list                           List API tokens
  token revoke <id>                    Revoke an API token

## Execution & Automation (paid plan)
  dispatch <id> [--priority] [--worker]   Queue an issue for execution as a job
  jobs [--bead=X]                         List recent jobs
  jobs show <id>                          Job details (JSON)
  jobs log <id> [--tail=N]               Job output log
  jobs failures [--bead=X]               Recent failed jobs
  analyze <id>                            Trigger sandbox analysis for an issue
  diagnose <id>                           Investigate: status, job history, failures
  score [<id>]                            Score beads for automability
  reconcile-tasks [--auto-fix] [--json]  Audit and fix backlog inconsistencies

## Integration (WIP — being reworked)
  sync                                 Trigger full GitHub reconciliation
  import <issue-number> | --all       Import GitHub issues (--state=open|all)

## Admin
  admin reset-user <user-id>           Reset a user's data (requires ADMIN_SECRET)

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (optional — for monitored workflows)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,
```

input.content:
# Update `help cli` and `prime` for tier-aware documentation

## Context

The `help cli` topic is significantly out of date — it documents ~20 of ~40+ commands. Meanwhile, execution/automation commands (dispatch, jobs, analyze, diagnose, score, reconcile-tasks) are going behind a paywall, and sync/import are being reworked as provider-based integrations. The `prime` command (agent context injection) should not teach agents about commands they can't use on the free tier.

This continues the pattern we established earlier this session by pulling `report` out of the default workflow.

## Changes

### 1. `internal/commands/help_topics.go` — Reorganize `"cli"` topic

Replace the flat "Commands" section (lines 36-95) with tiered sections:

- **Issue Tracking (Core)** — existing commands, unchanged text (preserves test assertions for `ready` and `show` descriptions)
- **Setup & Utility** — `login`, `init`, `doctor`, `version`, `self-update`, `uninstall`, `agent-setup`, `alias-setup`
- **Project Management** — `project create/list/delete`
- **Token Management** — `token create/list/revoke`
- **Execution & Automation (paid plan)** — `dispatch`, `jobs` (list/show/log/failures), `analyze`, `diagnose`, `score`, `reconcile-tasks`
- **Integration (WIP — being reworked)** — `sync`, `import`
- **Admin** — `admin reset-user`

ID Formats, --since Format, Priority, and Examples sections stay unchanged.

### 2. `internal/commands/prime.go` — Remove paid/WIP from JSON list

Line 141: Remove `jobs show`, `score <id>`, and `sync` from the "commands that return JSON" list. These are paid-tier or WIP. Keep `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `token list`.

No other prime.go changes needed — Quick Reference and On-Demand Reference are already clean from the earlier `report` removal.

### 3. `CLAUDE.md` — No changes

Already clean. No references to paid-tier or WIP commands.

## Test impact

- `TestHelpTopicCLI` (line 561): asserts `"CLI Reference"` — preserved in new header
- Test at line 667: asserts `"choose what to work on next"` — preserved verbatim in `ready` description
- Test at line 670: asserts `"Start here when the user already named the issue"` — preserved verbatim in `show` description

## Verification

1. `go build ./cmd/devdash` — compiles
2. `go test ./internal/commands/...` — all tests pass
3. `./devdash help cli` — visually confirm tiered sections
4. `./devdash prime` — confirm no paid/WIP commands in output

input.old_string:
"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference

## Commands
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress (optional, see: help report)

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  version                              Print version

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (optional — for monitored workflows)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,

"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference

## Issue Tracking (Core)
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress (optional, see: help report)

## Setup & Utility
  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  version                              Print version
  self-update                          Update devdash to the latest version
  uninstall [--dry-run]               Remove devdash and its configuration
  agent-setup [--agent=X] [--force]   Configure agent instructions for this repo
  alias-setup                          Add 'dd' alias to your shell RC file

## Project Management
  project create --name="..." [flags] Create a new project (--repo, --description)
  project list                         List all projects
  project delete <project-id>         Delete a project

## Token Management
  token create <name>                  Create a new API token
  token list                           List API tokens
  token revoke <id>                    Revoke an API token

## Execution & Automation (paid plan)
  dispatch <id> [--priority] [--worker]   Queue an issue for execution as a job
  jobs [--bead=X]                         List recent jobs
  jobs show <id>                          Job details (JSON)
  jobs log <id> [--tail=N]               Job output log
  jobs failures [--bead=X]               Recent failed jobs
  analyze <id>                            Trigger sandbox analysis for an issue
  diagnose <id>                           Investigate: status, job history, failures
  score [<id>]                            Score beads for automability
  reconcile-tasks [--auto-fix] [--json]  Audit and fix backlog inconsistencies

## Integration (WIP — being reworked)
  sync                                 Trigger full GitHub reconciliation
  import <issue-number> | --all       Import GitHub issues (--state=open|all)

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (optional — for monitored workflows)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,

toolUseResult.originalFile:
package commands

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func registerHelpTopics(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {
	rootCmd.SetHelpCommand(&cobra.Command{
		Use:   "help [topic]",
		Short: "Help about devdash or a specific topic",
		Long:  "Available topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report",
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			if len(args) == 0 {
				return rootCmd.Help()
			}

			if text, ok := helpTopics[args[0]]; ok {
				fmt.Println(text)
				return nil
			}

			target, _, err := rootCmd.Find(args)
			if err == nil && target != nil {
				return target.Help()
			}

			fmt.Printf("Unknown help topic: %s\n\nAvailable topics: cli, workflow, close, pr, projects, report\n", args[0])
			return nil
		},
	})
}

var helpTopics = map[string]string{
	"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference

## Commands
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress (optional, see: help report)

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  version                              Print version

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (optional — for monitored workflows)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,

	"workflow": `# DevDash Workflow Guide

## Starting Work
  devdash ready                             See what's available
  devdash show <id>                         Read the full issue
  devdash update <id> --status=in_progress  Mark as started

## Completing Work
  git add <files>
  git commit -m "message"
  git push
  devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never close before push.

## When to Create Issues
  - Before writing ANY code (issue-first rule)
  - One issue per commit
  - When scope expands mid-task, create a new issue
  - For multi-step plans: one parent + child issues per step
  - Even spikes/investigations deserve an issue

## Decomposition Patterns
  - Parent/child: "parts of a whole" (--parent=<id>)
  - Dependencies: "X must happen before Y" (dep add)
  - Prefer parent/child for breakdown, dependencies for ordering

## Before Starting Work
  Always run devdash show <id> and check:
  - parentBeadId: understand the larger goal
  - blockedBy/blocks: understand ordering constraints
  - preInstructions: agent-specific context`,

	"close": `# Close Summary Guide

Close summaries are institutional memory. Future agents and humans will
read them to understand what happened.

## What to Include
  - What changed (files, functions, behavior)
  - Why (the motivation, not just "fixed the bug")
  - Decisions made and alternatives considered
  - Surprises or gotchas discovered
  - Follow-up work needed

## Examples

  Good: "Added cursor-based pagination to FetchAll. Chose generic approach
  with type parameter to avoid duplication. API returns plain arrays for
  some endpoints — added fallback unmarshaling."

  Bad: "Done"
  Bad: "Fixed the issue"
  Bad: "Implemented as described"

## Metadata
  --summary="..."        What changed and why
  --commit=SHA           Git commit SHA
  --pr=URL               Pull request URL (if applicable)`,

	"pr": "# Pull Request Format\n\n## DevDash Footer\nEvery PR should include a DevDash footer section:\n\n  ## DevDash\n  Project: `95ca3de0-7e4f-4f9e-9b17-36f5609cfa11`\n  Issues:\n  - [<issue-id>](https://dev-dash-blue.vercel.app/issue/<issue-id>)\n\nReplace <issue-id> with the full UUID of each devdash issue.\nIf the PR addresses multiple issues, list each on its own line.",

	"projects": `# Cross-Project Work

## Targeting a Different Project
  DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>

## Finding Beads Across Projects
  devdash find <full-uuid>

  Returns bead with project context (projectId, projectName).
  Requires full UUID — prefix lookup only works within a project.

## Cross-Project Dependencies
  Dependencies work across projects. The blocker bead is resolved globally
  (not project-scoped), so you can link issues from different projects.

  Use full UUIDs for cross-project bead references:
    devdash dep add <issue-uuid> <blocker-uuid-from-other-project>

  The server verifies you have access to both projects.`,

	"report": `# Progress Reporting (Optional)

The report command is NOT part of the default workflow. The core workflow is:
  create → update --status=in_progress → [work] → close

Report exists for clients that want granular, real-time progress tracking —
dashboards, CI bots, multi-agent orchestrators, or any system polling for
intermediate status between "in progress" and "closed".

## When to Use Report
  - A monitoring dashboard is watching issue progress
  - Multiple agents coordinate and need to signal milestones to each other
  - Long-running tasks where a human or system needs visibility before completion
  - Error reporting when work is abandoned mid-task

  If none of these apply, you don't need report. Just close the issue when done.

## Status Values
  code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
  committed       Git commit created
  pushed          Pushed to remote
  error           Something went wrong

## Usage
  devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
  devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
  devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
  devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"

  Report is fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.

## For Client Developers
  Report statuses are available in the API for any client to consume.
  Clients that want agents to report should include reporting instructions
  in their agent context (e.g., via prime output or custom agent prompts).
  The default prime output does not include reporting instructions.`,
}

package commands

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/jasonmassey/devdash-cli-go/internal/api"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

func newPrimeCmd(d *Deps) *cobra.Command {
	return &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "prime",
		Short: "Output AI-optimized workflow context for agent injection",
		Long: `Output a full AI-optimized workflow context block designed for agent injection.

The output includes the current project info, team roster, all known projects,
health stats (open/in-progress/blocked counts), mandatory rules, a quick
reference for common workflows, and on-demand help topics.

Run this at the start of every new session, after context compaction, or
whenever the agent has lost track of project state. The output is formatted
as Markdown so it can be injected directly into an agent's context window.`,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			pid, err := d.requireProject(cmd)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}

			projData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid)
			var project api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(projData, &project)

			allProjData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects")
			var allProjects []api.Project
			_ = json.Unmarshal(allProjData, &allProjects)

			beads, _ := api.FetchAll[api.Bead](d.Client, "/beads?projectId="+pid)

			teamData, _ := d.Client.Get("/projects/" + pid + "/members?format=compact")
			var members []api.TeamMember
			_ = json.Unmarshal(teamData, &members)

			var open, inProgress, blocked int
			completedIDs := make(map[string]bool)
			for _, b := range beads {
				switch b.Status {
				case "pending":
					open++
				case "in_progress":
					inProgress++
				case "completed":
					completedIDs[b.ID] = true
				}
			}
			for _, b := range beads {
				if b.Status == "pending" && len(b.BlockedBy) > 0 && isBlocked(b, completedIDs) {
					blocked++
				}
			}

			fmt.Println("# Dev-Dash Workflow Context")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("> **Context Recovery**: Run `devdash prime` after compaction, clear, or new session.")
			fmt.Println("> Use `devdash` (dev-dash CLI) for ALL task tracking — never `bd`.")
			fmt.Println()

			repo := ""
			if project.GithubRepo != "" {
				repo = fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", project.GithubRepo)
			}
			fmt.Printf("**Project**: %s%s  |  **ID**: `%s`\n", project.Name, repo, shortID(project.ID))
			fmt.Printf("**Health**: %d open, %d in progress, %d blocked\n", open, inProgress, blocked)
			fmt.Println()

			if len(members) > 0 {
				fmt.Println("## Team")
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("| Name | Username | Email | Role |")
				fmt.Println("|------|----------|-------|------|")
				cap := 20
				if len(members) < cap {
					cap = len(members)
				}
				for _, m := range members[:cap] {
					name := m.Name
					if name == "" {
						name = m.Email
					}
					username := m.Username
					if username != "" {
						username = "@" + username
					}
					fmt.Printf("| %s | %s | %s | %s |\n", name, username, m.Email, m.Role)
				}
				fmt.Println()
				fmt.Println("Assign with: `devdash update <id> --owner=<email-or-name>`")
				fmt.Println()
			}

			fmt.Println("## All Projects")
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("| Short | Full UUID | Name | Repo |")
			fmt.Println("|-------|-----------|------|------|")
			for _, p := range allProjects {
				r := "(no repo)"
				if p.GithubRepo != "" {
					r = fmt.Sprintf("(%s)", p.GithubRepo)
				}
				fmt.Printf("| `%s` | `%s` | %s | %s |\n", shortID(p.ID), p.ID, p.Name, r)
			}
			fmt.Println()
			fmt.Println("Use `DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>` to target a specific project.")
			fmt.Println("Short prefixes work for project IDs too: `DD_PROJECT_ID=47eb046a devdash ready`")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Rules (MANDATORY)")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-first**: Create a devdash issue BEFORE writing code. No exceptions.")
			fmt.Println("- **Issue-per-commit**: Every git commit must map to a devdash issue. If scope expands, create new issues.")
			fmt.Println("- **Mark in-progress**: `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work.")
			fmt.Println("- **Pre-commit checkpoint**: Before each `git commit`, verify you have an issue. If not, create one.")
			fmt.Println("- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.")
			fmt.Println("- **No orphaned work**: At session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.")
			exe, _ := os.Executable()
			fmt.Printf("- **Path**: `%s`\n", exe)
			fmt.Println("- **Preserve stderr**: Avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands — stderr contains error details you'll need for debugging failures.")
			fmt.Println("- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use `bd`, TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Quick Reference")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (task already named)**: `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Start (need a task)**: `devdash ready` → `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`")
			fmt.Println("- **Complete**: git add → commit → push → `devdash close <id> --summary=\"...\" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`")
			fmt.Println("- Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.")
			fmt.Println("- One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## Output Formats")
			fmt.Println("- There is no `--json` flag. Do not try `--json`, `--format`, or `--output`.")
			fmt.Println("- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`")
			fmt.Println("- All other commands return human-readable text. Parse UUIDs from `create`/`close`/`update` output directly.")
			fmt.Println()

			fmt.Println("## On-Demand Reference")
			fmt.Println("Run these when you need detailed guidance:")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs")
			fmt.Println("- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work")

			return nil
		},
	}
}

content.content:
# DevDash CLI Reference

## Issue Tracking (Core)
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress (optional, see: help report)

## Setup & Utility
  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  version                              Print version
  self-update                          Update devdash to the latest version
  uninstall [--dry-run]               Remove devdash and its configuration
  agent-setup [--agent=X] [--force]   Configure agent instructions for this repo
  alias-setup                          Add 'dd' alias to your shell RC file

## Project Management
  project create --name="..." [flags] Create a new project (--repo, --description)
  project list                         List all projects
  project delete <project-id>         Delete a project

## Token Management
  token create <name>                  Create a new API token
  token list                           List API tokens
  token revoke <id>                    Revoke an API token

## Execution & Automation (paid plan)
  dispatch <id> [--priority] [--worker]   Queue an issue for execution as a job
  jobs [--bead=X]                         List recent jobs
  jobs show <id>                          Job details (JSON)
  jobs log <id> [--tail=N]               Job output log
  jobs failures [--bead=X]               Recent failed jobs
  analyze <id>                            Trigger sandbox analysis for an issue
  diagnose <id>                           Investigate: status, job history, failures
  score [<id>]                            Score beads for automability
  reconcile-tasks [--auto-fix] [--json]  Audit and fix backlog inconsistencies

## Integration (WIP — being reworked)
  sync                                 Trigger full GitHub reconciliation
  import <issue-number> | --all       Import GitHub issues (--state=open|all)

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (optional — for monitored workflows)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"

1	<!-- devdash:agent-instructions -->
2	
3	# DevDash — AI Agent Task Tracking
4	
5	This project uses **devdash** (`dd`) for task tracking. DevDash is a shared memory
6	between you and the user — a place where ideas, decisions, and progress are captured so
7	nothing gets lost.
8	
9	Run devdash commands yourself via the terminal — do not just tell the user to run them.
10	Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, `bd`, or markdown files for tracking. When the user
11	says "dd", they mean the devdash CLI, not the Unix `dd` data-copy utility.
12	
13	Issues are called "beads" internally. You'll see this in fields like `parentBeadId`.
14	
15	## Core Principles
16	
17	**Be a capture reflex.**
18	When the user mentions a bug, idea, TODO, or "we should probably..." — offer to create
19	an issue. Don't wait to be asked.
20	
21	**Issue-first.**
22	Create an issue before doing work. Your first action when asked to implement something
23	should be `devdash create`.
24	
25	**One issue per logical unit of work.**
26	If a task has multiple steps, create a parent issue and child issues. Scope creep during
27	a task = new issue, not an expanded current one. Every git commit must map to a devdash
28	issue.
29	
30	## Rules
31	
32	1. Create an issue before starting work. No exceptions.
33	2. `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work on an issue.
34	3. Close with a substantive summary — write it for a future reader with zero context.
35	4. Don't batch unrelated work into a single issue.
36	- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.
37	6. No orphaned work: at session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.
38	7. Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never run git and devdash close in parallel.
39	8. Preserve stderr: avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands.
40	
41	## Completing Work
42	
43	`git add` → `git commit` → `git push` → `devdash close <id>`
44	On successful completions: `devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`.
45	If a PR exists, include `--pr=URL` too.
46	Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.
47	One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.
48	
49	## On-Demand Reference
50	
51	Run these when you need detailed guidance:
52	- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)
53	- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
54	- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples
55	- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
56	- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work
57	
58	## Agent-Specific Instructions
59	
60	- You may use your built-in task tools (TaskCreate, TodoWrite, etc.) for your own tracking, but you **must also** create and update devdash issues. Devdash is the system of record.
61	- When the user asks you to implement a plan, feature, or fix: your **very first action** is `devdash create`. Do not read files, do not write code — create the issue first.
62	- For multi-step plans, create one devdash issue per step before starting any implementation. Group them under a parent issue. Then work through them sequentially: mark in-progress, implement, commit, close, move to next.
63	- After creating issues, follow the normal workflow: mark in-progress, do the work, commit, then close.
64	<!-- /devdash:agent-instructions -->
65

<!-- devdash:agent-instructions -->

# DevDash — AI Agent Task Tracking

This project uses **devdash** (`dd`) for task tracking. DevDash is a shared memory
between you and the user — a place where ideas, decisions, and progress are captured so
nothing gets lost.

Run devdash commands yourself via the terminal — do not just tell the user to run them.
Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, `bd`, or markdown files for tracking. When the user
says "dd", they mean the devdash CLI, not the Unix `dd` data-copy utility.

Issues are called "beads" internally. You'll see this in fields like `parentBeadId`.

## Core Principles

**Be a capture reflex.**
When the user mentions a bug, idea, TODO, or "we should probably..." — offer to create
an issue. Don't wait to be asked.

**Issue-first.**
Create an issue before doing work. Your first action when asked to implement something
should be `devdash create`.

**One issue per logical unit of work.**
If a task has multiple steps, create a parent issue and child issues. Scope creep during
a task = new issue, not an expanded current one. Every git commit must map to a devdash
issue.

## Rules

1. Create an issue before starting work. No exceptions.
2. `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work on an issue.
3. Close with a substantive summary — write it for a future reader with zero context.
4. Don't batch unrelated work into a single issue.
- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.
6. No orphaned work: at session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.
7. Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never run git and devdash close in parallel.
8. Preserve stderr: avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands.

## Completing Work

`git add` → `git commit` → `git push` → `devdash close <id>`
On successful completions: `devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`.
If a PR exists, include `--pr=URL` too.
Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.
One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.

## On-Demand Reference

Run these when you need detailed guidance:
- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)
- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples
- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work

## Agent-Specific Instructions

- You may use your built-in task tools (TaskCreate, TodoWrite, etc.) for your own tracking, but you **must also** create and update devdash issues. Devdash is the system of record.
- When the user asks you to implement a plan, feature, or fix: your **very first action** is `devdash create`. Do not read files, do not write code — create the issue first.
- For multi-step plans, create one devdash issue per step before starting any implementation. Group them under a parent issue. Then work through them sequentially: mark in-progress, implement, commit, close, move to next.
- After creating issues, follow the normal workflow: mark in-progress, do the work, commit, then close.
<!-- /devdash:agent-instructions -->

content.text:
Interesting — the installed `prime` binary still has the old output (pre-our changes). But comparing the two documents, here's what I see:

**In CLAUDE.md but NOT in `prime`:**
| Instruction | In CLAUDE.md | In prime |
|---|---|---|
| "Be a capture reflex" — offer to create issues proactively | Yes | No |
| "dd" means devdash CLI, not Unix dd | Yes | No |
| "beads" internal terminology explanation | Yes | No |
| Git ops MUST succeed before closing — never run git and close in parallel | Yes | No (has "close after push" but not the parallel warning) |
| Close with `--pr=URL` when applicable | Yes | No |
| Multi-step = parent + children (decomposition guidance) | Yes | Partial (one-liner) |
| On-demand reference to `help workflow` | Yes | Yes |
| Agent-specific: first action is `devdash create` | Yes | No |
| Agent-specific: built-in task tools OK but devdash is system of record | Yes | No |

**In `prime` but NOT in CLAUDE.md:**
| Instruction | In prime | In CLAUDE.md |
|---|---|---|
| Project/team/health context | Yes | No (makes sense, dynamic) |
| "Pre-commit checkpoint" rule | Yes | No |
| Output format guidance (no --json, which commands return JSON) | Yes | No |
| Binary path | Yes | No |
| Cross-project targeting via DD_PROJECT_ID | Yes | No |

**Notable gaps for client-facing instructions:**

1. **"Capture reflex"** — This is a powerful behavioral instruction. Agents should proactively offer to create issues when users mention bugs/TODOs. This belongs in the default agent-setup output, not just our own CLAUDE.md.

2. **"Never run git and close in parallel"** — `prime` says "close after push" but doesn't warn about parallelism. Agents commonly batch tool calls. This is a real foot-gun.

3. **"First action is devdash create"** — The agent-specific section in CLAUDE.md is more prescriptive than prime's rules. Prime says "issue-first" but doesn't explicitly say "your very first action is create, before reading files."

4. **`--pr=URL` on close** — Not mentioned in prime at all.

5. **"dd" disambiguation** — Prime says "never `bd`" but doesn't handle the `dd` Unix confusion.

Should I check what `agent-setup` actually writes to see if it's closer to CLAUDE.md or prime?

message.content: yes

content.content:
Configure AI agent instruction files for the current repository so that
agents automatically follow the devdash workflow.

By default the command auto-detects which agents are present (looking for
CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md) and writes devdash
instructions into each. Use --agent to target specific agents by name, and
--force to overwrite configs that already contain devdash instructions.

The --close-on flag controls the workflow gate: set it to "commit" or "push"
to determine when agents are allowed to close issues.

Usage:
  devdash agent-setup [flags]

Flags:
      --agent string      Comma-separated agent names
      --all               Setup all detected agents
      --close-on string   Workflow gate: commit or push (default "push")
      --force             Overwrite existing configs
  -h, --help              help for agent-setup

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

content.text:
This is revealing. Three different levels of instruction quality:

| | CLAUDE.md (ours) | `agent-setup` → CLAUDE.md | `agent-setup` → AGENTS.md |
|---|---|---|---|
| Capture reflex | Yes | No | No |
| dd disambiguation | Yes | No | No |
| "beads" terminology | Yes | No | No |
| Issue-first (create before anything) | Yes | Minimal ("before writing code") | Yes |
| Mark in-progress | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Close after push | Yes | Yes (configurable) | Yes (configurable) |
| No parallel git+close | Yes | No | No |
| `--pr=URL` on close | Yes | No | No |
| `--commit=SHA` on close | Yes | Yes | No |
| Close summaries guidance | Yes | No | No |
| Scope creep = new issue | Yes | No | No |
| Multi-step = parent + children | Yes | No | No |
| On-demand help references | Yes | No | No |
| Prime at session start | No | No | Yes |
| Report at milestones | Removed | No | Yes (still there!) |
| Output format guidance | No | No | No |
| Minimize redundant startup | No | No | Yes |
| Targeted reads over exploration | No | No | Yes |

A few things jump out:

1. **The Claude template (line 103-117) is extremely bare** compared to both our CLAUDE.md and the Codex template. It's missing most of the behavioral guidance that makes agents effective.

2. **The Codex template (line 145-166) is much richer** but has its own issues — it still tells agents to `devdash report` at milestones (line 162), which contradicts the decision we just made.

3. **Neither template includes** the capture reflex, close summary guidance, scope creep rule, parent/child decomposition, on-demand help references, or the parallel execution warning.

4. **No copilot template exists** — the `setupAgent` switch (line 81) doesn't have a case for it despite detecting it.

Feels like the Claude template needs to be brought up to parity with our handcrafted CLAUDE.md, and the Codex template needs the `report` line removed. Want me to create issues for these?

message.content: yes please

input.command: devdash create --title="Enrich agent-setup Claude template to match handcrafted CLAUDE.md quality" --description="The setupClaude template (agent_setup.go:103-117) is extremely bare — just 5 rules and a workflow one-liner. It's missing: capture reflex, dd disambiguation, beads terminology, no-parallel-git-close warning, --pr=URL on close, close summary guidance, scope creep = new issue, parent/child decomposition, on-demand help references. Bring it up to parity with the handcrafted CLAUDE.md in this repo." 2>&1

 var helpTopics = map[string]string{
 	"cli": `# DevDash CLI Reference
 
-## Commands
+## Issue Tracking (Core)
   ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
   list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
   blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
@@ -48,7 +48,6 @@ var helpTopics = map[string]string{
   update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
   close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
   delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
-  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress
 
   dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
   dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
@@ -56,12 +55,44 @@ var helpTopics = map[string]string{
   comments <id>                        List comments
   activity [<id>]                      View activity log
 
+  team                                 List team members
+  prime                                Output agent workflow context
+  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress (optional, see: help report)
+
+## Setup & Utility
   login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
   init                                 Initialize project in current repo
   doctor                               Check configuration
-  team                                 List team members
-  prime                                Output agent workflow context
   version                              Print version
+  self-update                          Update devdash to the latest version
+  uninstall [--dry-run]               Remove devdash and its configuration
+  agent-setup [--agent=X] [--force]   Configure agent instructions for this repo
+  alias-setup                          Add 'dd' alias to your shell RC file
+
+## Project Management
+  project create --name="..." [flags] Create a new project (--repo, --description)
+  project list                         List all projects
+  project delete <project-id>         Delete a project
+
+## Token Management
+  token create <name>                  Create a new API token
+  token list                           List API tokens
+  token revoke <id>                    Revoke an API token
+
+## Execution & Automation (paid plan)
+  dispatch <id> [--priority] [--worker]   Queue an issue for execution as a job
+  jobs [--bead=X]                         List recent jobs
+  jobs show <id>                          Job details (JSON)
+  jobs log <id> [--tail=N]               Job output log
+  jobs failures [--bead=X]               Recent failed jobs
+  analyze <id>                            Trigger sandbox analysis for an issue
+  diagnose <id>                           Investigate: status, job history, failures
+  score [<id>]                            Score beads for automability
+  reconcile-tasks [--auto-fix] [--json]  Audit and fix backlog inconsistencies
+
+## Integration (WIP — being reworked)
+  sync                                 Trigger full GitHub reconciliation
+  import <issue-number> | --all       Import GitHub issues (--state=open|all)
 
 ## ID Formats
   Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
@@ -91,7 +122,7 @@ var helpTopics = map[string]string{
   # Create a child issue under a parent
   devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123
 
-  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
+  # Report progress (optional — for monitored workflows)
   devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"`,
 
 	"workflow": `# DevDash Workflow Guide
@@ -176,11 +207,22 @@ read them to understand what happened.
 
   The server verifies you have access to both projects.`,
 
-	"report": `# Progress Reporting
+	"report": `# Progress Reporting (Optional)
 
-## Why Report
-  Reporting prevents issues from appearing stale (30+ min without activity).
-  It's fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.
+The report command is NOT part of the default workflow. The core workflow is:
+  create → update --status=in_progress → [work] → close
+
+Report exists for clients that want granular, real-time progress tracking —
+dashboards, CI bots, multi-agent orchestrators, or any system polling for
+intermediate status between "in progress" and "closed".
+
+## When to Use Report
+  - A monitoring dashboard is watching issue progress
+  - Multiple agents coordinate and need to signal milestones to each other
+  - Long-running tasks where a human or system needs visibility before completion
+  - Error reporting when work is abandoned mid-task
+
+  If none of these apply, you don't need report. Just close the issue when done.
 
 ## Status Values
   code_complete   Code written, ready for commit
@@ -188,13 +230,17 @@ read them to understand what happened.
   pushed          Pushed to remote
   error           Something went wrong
 
-## When to Report
-  After completing code: devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
-  After commit:          devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
-  After push:            devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
-  On error:              devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"
+## Usage
+  devdash report <id> --status=code_complete --summary="..."
+  devdash report <id> --status=committed --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
+  devdash report <id> --status=pushed --branch=$(git branch --show-current)
+  devdash report <id> --status=error --error="description"
+
+  Report is fire-and-forget — if it fails, nothing breaks.
 
-## Always Report Before Exiting
-  Even if the work is incomplete, report the current status so the issue
-  doesn't appear abandoned.`,
+## For Client Developers
+  Report statuses are available in the API for any client to consume.
+  Clients that want agents to report should include reporting instructions
+  in their agent context (e.g., via prime output or custom agent prompts).
+  The default prime output does not include reporting instructions.`,
 }

git add internal/commands/help_topics.go internal/commands/prime.go CLAUDE.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Reorganize CLI docs by tier, make report optional

- Reorganize help cli from flat command list into tiered sections:
  Issue Tracking (Core), Setup & Utility, Project Management,
  Token Management, Execution & Automation (paid), Integration (WIP)
- Add 15+ previously undocumented commands to help cli
- Remove report from default agent workflow (prime, CLAUDE.md)
- Rewrite report help topic as opt-in for clients wanting granular
  progress tracking
- Remove paid/WIP commands (jobs show, score, sync) from prime
  JSON format list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

lines: - Run devdash commands yourself in the terminal; do not ask the user to run them for you.
- Before making code changes, make sure a devdash issue exists and is marked `+"`in_progress`"+`.
- Before each commit, confirm that the commit maps to exactly one devdash issue.
-- Report progress with `+"`devdash report`"+` at `+"`code_complete`"+`, `+"`committed`"+`, and `+"`pushed`"+`.
- Never close a devdash issue until `+"`git %s`"+` succeeds.
- Preserve existing user changes. Do not revert unrelated modifications or overwrite work you did not make.
- Run the narrowest verification that meaningfully covers the change, then summarize the result for the user.

input.new_string:
instructions := fmt.Sprintf(`<!-- devdash:agent-instructions -->

# DevDash — AI Agent Task Tracking

This project uses **devdash** (`+"`dd`"+`) for task tracking. DevDash is a shared memory
between you and the user — a place where ideas, decisions, and progress are captured so
nothing gets lost.

Run devdash commands yourself via the terminal — do not just tell the user to run them.
Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, `+"`bd`"+`, or markdown files for tracking. When the user
says "dd", they mean the devdash CLI, not the Unix `+"`dd`"+` data-copy utility.

Issues are called "beads" internally. You'll see this in fields like `+"`parentBeadId`"+`.

Project ID: %s

## Core Principles

**Be a capture reflex.**
When the user mentions a bug, idea, TODO, or "we should probably..." — offer to create
an issue. Don't wait to be asked.

**Issue-first.**
Create an issue before doing work. Your first action when asked to implement something
should be `+"`devdash create`"+`.

**One issue per logical unit of work.**
If a task has multiple steps, create a parent issue and child issues. Scope creep during
a task = new issue, not an expanded current one. Every git commit must map to a devdash
issue.

## Rules

1. Create an issue before starting work. No exceptions.
2. `+"`devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`"+` before starting work on an issue.
3. Close with a substantive summary — write it for a future reader with zero context.
4. Don't batch unrelated work into a single issue.
5. **Close after %s**: Only close issues after `+"`git %s`"+` succeeds — never before.
6. No orphaned work: at session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.
7. Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never run git and devdash close in parallel.
8. Preserve stderr: avoid `+"`2>/dev/null`"+` on devdash commands.

## Completing Work

`+"`git add`"+` → `+"`git commit`"+` → `+"`git %s`"+` → `+"`devdash close <id>`"+`
On successful completions: `+"`devdash close <id> --summary=\"...\" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`"+`.
If a PR exists, include `+"`--pr=URL`"+` too.
Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.
One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.

## On-Demand Reference

Run these when you need detailed guidance:
- `+"`devdash help cli`"+` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)
- `+"`devdash help workflow`"+` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
- `+"`devdash help close`"+` — Close summary expectations with examples
- `+"`devdash help pr`"+` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
- `+"`devdash help projects`"+` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work

## Agent-Specific Instructions

- You may use your built-in task tools (TaskCreate, TodoWrite, etc.) for your own tracking, but you **must also** create and update devdash issues. Devdash is the system of record.
- When the user asks you to implement a plan, feature, or fix: your **very first action** is `+"`devdash create`"+`. Do not read files, do not write code — create the issue first.
- For multi-step plans, create one devdash issue per step before starting any implementation. Group them under a parent issue. Then work through them sequentially: mark in-progress, implement, commit, close, move to next.
- After creating issues, follow the normal workflow: mark in-progress, do the work, commit, then close.
<!-- /devdash:agent-instructions -->
`, pid, closeOn, closeOn, closeOn)

## Rules

1. Create an issue before starting work. No exceptions.
2. `+"`devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`"+` before starting work on an issue.
3. Close with a substantive summary — write it for a future reader with zero context.
4. Don't batch unrelated work into a single issue.
5. **Close after %s**: Only close issues after `+"`git %s`"+` succeeds — never before.
6. No orphaned work: at session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.
7. Git operations MUST succeed before closing. Never run git and devdash close in parallel.
8. Preserve stderr: avoid `+"`2>/dev/null`"+` on devdash commands.

## Completing Work

`+"`git add`"+` → `+"`git commit`"+` → `+"`git %s`"+` → `+"`devdash close <id>`"+`
On successful completions: `+"`devdash close <id> --summary=\"...\" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`"+`.
If a PR exists, include `+"`--pr=URL`"+` too.
Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.
One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.

## On-Demand Reference

Run these when you need detailed guidance:
- `+"`devdash help cli`"+` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)
- `+"`devdash help workflow`"+` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
- `+"`devdash help close`"+` — Close summary expectations with examples
- `+"`devdash help pr`"+` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
- `+"`devdash help projects`"+` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work

## Agent-Specific Instructions

- You may use your built-in task tools (TaskCreate, TodoWrite, etc.) for your own tracking, but you **must also** create and update devdash issues. Devdash is the system of record.
- When the user asks you to implement a plan, feature, or fix: your **very first action** is `+"`devdash create`"+`. Do not read files, do not write code — create the issue first.
- For multi-step plans, create one devdash issue per step before starting any implementation. Group them under a parent issue. Then work through them sequentially: mark in-progress, implement, commit, close, move to next.
- After creating issues, follow the normal workflow: mark in-progress, do the work, commit, then close.
<!-- /devdash:agent-instructions -->
`, pid, closeOn, closeOn, closeOn)

	var content []byte

	} else {
		content = []byte(instructions)
